mainEntity.name: Could a bad mobile experience on my site be the reason I’m not showing up in Google AI Overviews?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Poor mobile experience can seriously hurt your chances of being cited in Google AI Overviews, because most searches and AI usage happen on mobile and Google favors mobile-friendly pages. If your site is hard to use on a phone, it’s a risk factor.

Issues like tiny text, slow load times, intrusive pop-ups, and broken layouts make your content less accessible both to users and to Google’s mobile-first indexing. AI Overviews are built on pages that deliver solid experience across devices.

Key factors:
- Responsive design that works on small screens
- Fast mobile load times and optimized images
- Minimal intrusive interstitials and pop-ups
- Easy-to-tap navigation and clear headings

Practically, test your key pages on multiple phones, use Google’s mobile-friendly and speed tools, and fix issues before investing heavily in AI Overview optimization. Good content won’t be fully rewarded if the mobile experience is poor.

Soft positioning: Treat mobile usability as a gatekeeper—without clearing it, AI Overview visibility is much harder to achieve.
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes. Since Google is mobile‑first, a slow or hard‑to‑use mobile site can significantly reduce your chances of appearing in AI Overviews. Fix responsiveness, speed, and usability issues before expecting strong AI Overview visibility.
